procedures.name: Lumbar puncture (diagnostic)
procedures.notes: Performed as part of the fever / altered-mental-status workup. CSF: normal cell count, normal protein/glucose, Gram stain and cultures negative; no evidence of CNS infection. Does not explain the fevers or unresponsiveness.

procedures.name: Repeat diagnostic paracentesis
procedures.notes: Repeated during the fever workup to re-evaluate for SBP. PMN <250, no organisms, cultures negative — NEGATIVE for spontaneous bacterial peritonitis.

procedures.name: Endotracheal intubation
procedures.notes: For airway protection in the setting of massive hematemesis.

procedures.name: Transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS), emergent
procedures.notes: Emergent TIPS for variceal hemorrhage refractory to endoscopic banding. Bleeding resolved. Patent shunt confirmed on post-procedure Doppler. (A functioning portosystemic shunt increases hepatic-encephalopathy risk — relevant to her subsequent altered mental status.)

procedures.name: Upper endoscopy (EGD) with attempted variceal band ligation
procedures.notes: Bedside EGD showed actively bleeding esophageal varices; band ligation attempted but did not achieve hemostasis → escalated to emergent TIPS.

procedures.name: Diagnostic paracentesis (admission)
procedures.notes: Admission diagnostic tap of new ascites: 86 PMN/uL (below the 250 threshold), no organisms on Gram stain — NEGATIVE for spontaneous bacterial peritonitis. SAAG >1.1 (portal hypertension).
